english: back? Dzl.; mi btúb-pa (mi btub pa) very frq. not to be able to prevail upon one's self, to be unable, also: to be unwilling, to have no mind (to do a thing).
Ld. *stúb-če* (stub ce), = °túb-pa ('thub pa), to cut to pieces, to cut up, meat, wood etc.; in W. also to mince; (in C. btsáb-pa (btsab pa)); gtubs-spyád (gtubs spyad) chopper Sch.

1. ferocity, rage; also adj. furious; kró-žiṅ gtúm-la snyiṅ-rje-med (khro zhing gtum la snying rje med) in furious wrath, merciless Dzl.; gdúg-čiṅ gtúm-pai klu (gdug cing gtum pai klu) a Lu in a deadly rage Sambh.; gtúm-pai sgra sgróg-pa (gtum pai sgra sgrog pa) to roar furiously Ith.; kró-gtum-pa (khro gtum pa) furious with rage Glr.; gtúm-žiṅ rgód-pa (gtum zhing rgod pa) obstinate and unmanageable, of a boy; gtúm-po (gtum po) Mil., gtúm-čan (gtum can), gtum-ldán (gtum ldan) cruel, fierce, furious Cs.; blá-ma tugs-gtúm-po jón (bla ma thugs gtum po byon) the Lama grows angry Mil. nt.; gtúm-mo (gtum mo) fem. a fury of a woman Dzl. 200, 10; Sch. also: hangman (?); rluṅ gtúm-mo (rlung gtum mo) Cs. a furious wind, a hurricane — 2. = btúm-pa (btum pa), °túm-pa ('thum pa), to veil, to cover; to wrap up, e.g. the head; with the instr. to wrap up or cover with a thing.

1. v. gtúm-pa (gtum pa) 1. — 2. in the more developed mysticism the power which meditating saints by dint of long continued practice may acquire of holding back their breath for a great length of time, by which means the air is supposed to be drawn from the ró-ma (ro ma) and rkyáṅ-ma (rkyang ma) (two veins, v. rtsá-ba (rtsa ba)) into the dbú-ma (dbu ma) (sróg-rtsa (srog rtsa), dhú-ti (dhu ti), aorta?) thus causing a feeling of uncommon warmth, comfort, and lightness inside, and finally even emancipating the body from the laws of gravity, so as to lift it up and hold it freely suspended in the air, Mil. frq.; v. also Tar. 186, 20; gtúm-poi bde-dród (gtum poi bde drod) the feeling of warmth just mentioned Mil.; gtúm-po °bar (gtum po 'bar) the warmth of meditation commences Mil. The three above-named veins are symbolically represented by a-shád (a shad), i. e. the second half of aṅ ཨང (ang), viz. ང (ng), hence a-šad-gtúm-po (a shad gtum po) the three veins'-meditation-warmth, Mil. -- 3. n. of the goddess Durga or Uma.
Lex. w.e.; Cs. bag, sack, wallet.
to grind, to pulverize, colours, medicinal substances etc.; cf. °tág-pa ('thag pa).
W. (Ld. *sté-pa* (ste pa)) gté-ba (gte ba), gté-ma (gte ma) C., gtéṅ-pa (gteng pa) Lexx., pawn, pledge, bail (Sch. also: a present); cf. gtá-ma (gta' ma); gteu (gteu) id.? hostage? Tar.

1. treasure, frq. -- 2. symb. num. for 9. -- gter-mdzód (gter mdzod) a treasury. — gtér-ka (gter ka) a mineral vein, mine, nór-gyi gtér-ka rnyéd-pa (nor gyi gter ka rnyed pa) to find a mine of precious metals.
Lt., Thgy. a magic ceremony for the purpose of averting misfortune; gto-bcós (gto bcos) id.
1. also btóg-pa (btog pa), °tóg-pa ('thog pa), to pluck off, gather, crop, tear out (one's hair) Lex. -- 2. v. se-gol (se gol).

to belong, appertain to; belonging, rgyál-poï gdúṅ-la gtógs-pa yin (rgyal po'i gdung la gtogs pa yin) you belong to the royal blood or family Dzl.; deï náṅ-du mi gtógs-sam (de'i nang du mi gtogs sam) am I not included in them? Dzl.; dzam-buï-glíṅ-la gtógs-pa (dzam bu'i gling la gtogs pa) belonging to Dzambuling Glr.; *di lé-ka daṅ ma tog* (di le ka dang ma tog) W. do not meddle with that! ma-gtógs-pa (ma gtogs pa), gen. adv. ma gtógs-par (ma gtogs par) except, besides. — gtogs-°dód (gtogs 'dod) Sch.: 'to love, to like, to wish; a good-for-nothing fellow'(?).

pf. btaṅ (btang), fut. gtaṅ (gtang), imp. toṅ (tong) (W. *taṅ-če* (tang ce), imp. *toṅ* (tong)) 1. to let a. to let go, to permit to go, to dismiss, čiï pyír bdág-čag-rnams-kyis gtoṅ (ci'i phyir bdag cag rnams kyis gtong) why should we let you go, suffer you (our teacher) to go? to let escape (a prisoner) Dzl.; to let loose (a dog against a person) Mil.; to let go, to quit one's hold ma gtoṅ (ma gtong), col. *ma taṅ* (ma tang) don't let him go, stop him! to leave, abandon, renounce, čos (chos) one's religion; more definitely: blos gtóṅ-ba (blos gtong ba), v. blo (blo); yóṅs-su gtóṅ-ba (yongs su gtong ba) to abandon altogether Dzl.; to leave off, to abstain from, gsód-par byá-ba gtóṅ-ba (gsod par bya ba gtong ba) to leave off killing Dzl. b. to let in, to admit, sgó-nas (sgo nas) through
